entries.title: The U.S. is imposing a 25% tariff on certain Brazilian goods, following a yearlong investigation by trade officials into what they described as unfair trade practices
A complex web of funds and $781,948,878 in donations—that we know of. This is the mystery money powering Trump’s second term.
Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin and his acting ICE director made the decision to halt most vehicle stops by federal immigration officials but hadn’t briefed Trump. He overruled them
The Trump administration is considering placing a bond of $100,000 on some green-card seekers applying at U.S. consulates abroad, according to people familiar with the plans

More than 100 House Democrats voted for a failed measure to eliminate more than $3 billion in military financing to Israel, proving a clear picture of how support for the country has cratered in the party
The Air Force for the first time successfully fired an air-to-air missile from a new jet-powered combat drone built by defense startup Anduril
Todd Blanche tried to ease concerns over a settlement between President Trump and the IRS that troubles key GOP senators whose support he needs to be confirmed as attorney general
Dr. Erica Schwartz, Trump’s nominee to lead the CDC, said she would uphold science amid lawmaker questions over vaccine policy and Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
The U.S. Mint is set to begin striking a dollar coin featuring President Trump’s face, a rare move to put a sitting president on American money
President Trump said U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers should continue using traffic stops to arrest immigrants, reversing a recent decision by the agency
The House passed legislation supported by President Trump to make daylight-saving time permanent year-round, advancing a longstanding effort to end the twice-a-year time switch nationwide
Billionaire Ken Griffin has already invested roughly $40 million into this year’s midterm campaigns, a total that could easily double as he seeks to shape the GOP’s future and help it in the midterms
President Trump is pushing Darline Graham, the sister of the late Sen. Lindsey Graham, to serve as an interim replacement in the Senate after the longtime lawmaker’s death
Michigan’s Democratic Senate primary will measure how far left the party is willing to go in the industrial heartland and beyond
The Supreme Court’s decision to reinstate Alabama’s congressional map has split the political future for many Black Democratic voters
The Democratic Party’s financial struggles have become a new test for Chairman Ken Martin as the party regroups from a bruising 2024 election loss
The hardest question for Trump administration nominees on Capitol Hill: Who won the 2020 presidential election?
The Pentagon plans to screen soldiers for testosterone deficiency as Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth continues his revamp of the country’s military
Neros, a startup started by former drone-racing teens, has secured a contract with the Defense Department worth up to $500 million to supply its drones to the Army
A lack of defensive capabilities aboard President Trump’s new jet led him to fly aboard an older Air Force One after U.S. officials received Israeli intelligence about a possible plot to kill him
Trump says he would consider selling F-35 jet fighters to Turkey, challenging years of congressional opposition
